items.title: The FDA is about to decide which peptides could soon become widely available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
items.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

items.title: AI stocks are echoing a 1990s market split. JPMorgan warns the next few weeks are critical.
items.description: The different price action between artificial-intelligence hyperscalers and chip and infrastructure stocks is a throwback to a setup in the late 1990s, argues JPMorgan.

items.title: Japan’s $1.8 trillion pension giant might bring money home. That could jolt U.S. stocks and the Fed.
items.description: Japan’s pension titan selling foreign assets could push U.S. yields up and sap demand for the dollar.

items.title: Lockheed Martin’s stock leaps as push to build more missiles faster pays off
items.description: Lockheed Martin’s stock jumps as a ramp up in missile production helped lead to an earnings beat and raise.

items.title: Oil nears $100 a barrel after Houthis claim strikes on Saudi Arabian tankers
items.description: Brent crude rose to over $98 a barrel following the news that the rebel group attacked the vessels in the Red Sea.

items.title: The U.S. premium for SK Hynix is set to stay after Korean regulatory ruling
items.description: The huge premium commanded by SK Hynix ‘s American depository receipts over its domestically-listed ordinary shares is likely to remain, after restrictions were put in place on the number of ADRs that

items.title: I want to transfer $17,000 in credit-card debt. Why did Wells Fargo offer me only a $4,000 credit limit?
items.description: “I asked why the limit was so low, but they couldn’t give me an explanation.”

items.title: T-Mobile earnings rise as customers pour into premium plans
items.description: The wireless carrier has focused on amassing “higher quality accounts” after introducing new plans last year.

items.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
items.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

items.title: Why beat-and-raise earnings reports are no longer enough to push the stock market higher
items.description: The changing inflation landscape could spell trouble for corporate America.
